sections.paragraph:
-

(1) i.q. وصل to join, to connect together; whence אַצִּיל joint, אֵצֶל, אָצִיל No. 1, side, near; Arab. أَصْلُ a root, as that which joins a tree to the ground, أَصُلَ to put forth deep roots, prop. to be firmly joined to the ground; metaph. to be sprung from an ancient and noble stock; comp. אָצִיל No. 2.

(2) denom. from אֵצֶל prop. to put by the side, to separate (compare גָּנַב); hence followed by מִן to take away from, Num 11:17 to refuse, Ecc 2:10. Followed by לְ to reserve for any one, Gen 27:36 (comp. הִבְדִּיל and גָּרַם followed by לְ). (This signification may be taken from אָצַר, ר being softened into ל.)

Niphal, to be narrowed, Eze 42:6.

Hiphil, fut. וַיָּאצֶל i.q. Kal No. 2, Num 11:25.

The derivatives are given under Kal No. 1.
